meetings.title: Discussion with Bas
meetings.summary: Discussed Hormuz implications for MGen (Philippine power utility) with Bas ahead of client meeting. MGen operates 1.3GW coal fleet with 97% Indonesian sourcing (~$170M annual exposure), planning additional 1.2GW coal plant. Key insights: coal competitiveness improved by Hormuz but Indonesian export restrictions driving price floor up; 40% of coal contracts expire end-2026, new contracts likely at higher prices; renewables getting "new shine" but grid resilience challenges remain. Bas to share Wood Mackenzie Philippines report, LNG supply-demand data, and Vietnam LNG-to-power frameworks. Jamie to send latest Hormuz materials to Bas for comparison.

meetings.title: Catch-up with Gwen
meetings.summary: Gwen is considering extending her LOA through June/July and evaluating whether to take the Young Whiskey marketing role or return to consulting with flex arrangements. She needs lighter workload with WFH flexibility and flexible hours due to transport time between QC and BGC. Jamie advised that the marketing role requirements aren't as specialist as she fears and stressed the need for firm, rules-based flex arrangements rather than fluid week-to-week decisions. Gwen will reassess her family situation after returning from her trip tonight and define hard rules for work arrangements before making a final decision.

meetings.title: Discussion with Mari of JICA
meetings.summary: Jamie discussed JICA Philippines agriculture RFP with Mari, focusing on food value chain strengthening and supply chain waste reduction (20% rice crop wastage). The work aligns well with BCG's ongoing FCDO agriculture projects. Jamie committed to providing FCDO materials and hypotheses for Mari's pre-RFP meeting, then setting proposal direction after June 17 RFP release. Timeline constraints exist as Mari is busy first 1.5 weeks and Jamie goes on leave June 26-July 10. Potential conflict of interest firewalling with FCDO work may need addressing later.

meetings.title: Media interview prep — OFW report, key messages, and tough questions
meetings.summary: Jamie completed media interview coaching with Archie focused on the upcoming OFW report launch. Key decisions included establishing three Must-Win Attributes (expert, impactful, looks after country's development) and three key messages about the OFW experience being emotionally complex, having levers to make sacrifice worthwhile, and concrete interventions for return. Jamie practiced handling tough questions including methodology challenges, political comparisons, and accusations of painting too rosy a picture. Two video recordings were completed showing significant improvement in warmth, pacing, and executive presence. Jamie will receive post-coaching notes and must prepare for anticipated challenging questions while practicing new communication techniques daily.

meetings.title: Discussion with Eugenia
meetings.summary: Jamie discussed TFD report media strategy with Eugenia, planning broadcast interviews and op-ed placement. **Key decisions**: BusinessWorld remains default for op-ed placement; Jamie will front at least one broadcast interview after completing media training. **Timeline constraints**: Jamie leaves June 23rd, but Lance returns end of June to take over reviews. **Energy BD focus**: Post-call with Naomi prioritized Meralco over Aboitiz, targeting their distributed energy resources (DER) growth strategy including rooftop solar retail business. **Next steps**: Jamie to align with Anthony today on interview timeline, draft op-ed framework before leave, with Naomi researching BCG materials on downstream solar and B2C retail electricity.

meetings.title: Media outreach, coaching, and deck prep for upcoming briefing
meetings.summary: Team preparing for Thursday briefing with comprehensive media strategy including op-ed, live interviews, and social amplification. Media coaching completed for $800 (reimbursable), found valuable for executive presence training. Briefing deck ready with potential acknowledgment of new US-Iran deal. Knowledge management pain point identified - scattered files across OneDrives with no shared workspace. Perry to train team on shared directory structure for better collaboration.

meetings.title: New note
meetings.summary: Met with foundation team about straw innovation pilot funding - they require 50% co-funding and detailed concept note covering commercial model (straw harvest, mushroom growing, compost), philanthropic capital justification, and scaling pathway. Key commercial discussions underway with Manila tech company for mushroom supply and large carbon credit buyer for compost facility at 24 tons/day. Separately addressed FCDO budget scrutiny of £2.3M charges - need to capture unbilled investment days and justify blended rate structure across four workstreams (Negros/Ilo deepening, Bukidnon/Isabela expansion, DOF adaptation platform, capacity building). Foundation approval process allows up to SGD 1M monthly, above that quarterly board approval required.

meetings.title: Naomi discussion
meetings.summary: Jamie held a 1:1 debrief with Naomi (CDC) covering her performance over the past four weeks across DRR, Meralco/MGen, and related workstreams. Overall assessment is positive — no performance concerns — with notable strengths in intellectual curiosity and improved slide structuring, backed by praise from Julian and Anthony. Jamie strongly encouraged Naomi to give honest feedback on the Maxes case evaluation form, emphasizing real career implications for the PL and no blowback risk for Naomi. Development areas identified include investing time in keyboard shortcuts and switching note-taking from iPhone to laptop to reduce workflow friction.

meetings.title: Too Big workshop debrief — farmer engagement and right-of-way strategy
meetings.summary: The TuBig workshop went well with Mark keen on fast execution; BCG's role will be farmer engagement and price testing while TuBig handles landowner negotiations, with weekly working sessions planned from mid-July. The Heads of Terms document was scoped into five sections (context/objectives, target business model, project structure, phasing/scale-up, project financials) written as a non-binding private-to-public-sector commitment targeting the provincial government as signatory, with a milestone-based refinancing clause to be added if partners commit below full ambition. Milestone 3 draft is targeted for completion next week after the post check-in, with Jan doing heads-down work on the financial model rebuild and Negros farm visits. Key open questions include: how to size the TuBig irrigation component and test a community-only infrastructure model without bulk water, and whether Keynes & Stables can be secured as a second hub (otherwise sizing to one hub at ~100M/year). On return from leave, Jamie's priorities are DA meetings in Negros/Iloilo, Cherry partnership sessions, the Jerome working session, and convincing Gina to accept private sector co-financing for the NEF model.

meetings.title: Meralco BD
meetings.summary: The call with Marco and Anthony focused on sharpening the Meralco BD strategy around two core problems: revenue contraction from retail open access and rooftop solar (~80% driven by 100kW+ consumer defection), and a $5B capex program over five years under ERC rate review. The team aligned on anchoring as **transformation partners** rather than strategy advisors, leading with cost optimization (Aleander/Sembcorp model) and DR/VPP business-build (Octopus model), with AI positioned as an enabler rather than the headline. Andrew remains the best access point and Jamie will restart discussions soon with concrete examples and expert connections rather than a full perspective. MGEN renewables/dispatch optimization is on hold pending Marco's follow-up with their renewable CEO.

meetings.title: ASEAN power grid risk framework — ADB pitch prep and team sourcing
meetings.summary: ADB and MAS are commissioning a flagship guide on risk management for ASEAN Power Grid interconnector projects, with a draft due end of December 2026 and final by February 2027. A pre-proposal pitch is expected the week of 27 July or 3 August; Jamie will serve in a PL or capacity-support role behind Joel, who will quarterback pitch prep and lead the storyline with 2–3 CTMs per week. The critical gap is deep power-sector risk allocation expertise, particularly EU cross-border cost allocation practitioners; internal candidates (Marco, Pietro, Marco Toneguti, Andrew Clairholt, Phil Hirchan) are being assessed and a GLG-type search may supplement. On the ACE front, BCG's MOU has been approved by ACE's board and Jamie must present at ACE's management meeting on Friday 17 July to define BCG's specific APG support areas, positioning BCG as the orchestrator across ADB, ACE, and other actors given the fragmented landscape of independent studies.

meetings.title: TF Workshop:
meetings.summary: The TF workshop aligned on **sustainable rice systems** (Column 3) as the primary narrative anchor for the Temasek Foundation concept note, with climate (CO2 avoided) and farmer income uplift as co-benefits and secondary indicators. Key metrics agreed: additional tons of rice added to the system (pre- and post-farm gate), farmer income uplift, and CO2 avoided; financial sustainability of operators is a minimum requirement rather than a standalone KPI. The Year 1 pilot target was expanded to **1,000 hectares** funded by 2M SGD (1M Temasek + 1M co-funding) structured as a one-year project, with a Year 3 ambition of 25,000 hectares aligned to the 2030 high-climate-risk cropland threshold; additional funding for Years 2–3 will need to be raised in parallel. The MOU with Iloilo Provincial Government is in community review and is expected to be signed before the British Ambassador visit on **August 6**; Jaron (now Acting Provincial Agriculturist) will be engaged Thursday to confirm timeline and baseline data. Open questions include: final clarification of Temasek's CapEx/OpEx and in-kind co-funding rules, how operator financial sustainability will be formally measured, and which national government agencies to invite to the IBC event.

meetings.title: Discussion on Hannah for interim CDC wit Heidi Xu
meetings.summary: Jamie had a quick check-in with Heidi Xu on Hannah (SA since Aug 2025, up for promotion to C). Heidi assessed Hannah as ready for C, citing strong client-facing communication and clean quantitative outputs as key strengths. The two development areas flagged are: (1) speed to output — sometimes taking several hours per slide, and needing to flag blockers earlier, which is especially critical in fast-turnaround due diligence work; and (2) sharpening executive insight / the 'so what,' likely linked to the speed issue. Jamie will pass this feedback to Hannah and confirm Heidi's evaluation form clears in the system before the CDC discussion.

meetings.title: ADB earthquake resilience pitch — approach, technical delivery, and implementation model
meetings.summary: BCG Manila is pitching ADB on a 12-week earthquake resilience master plan engagement (Tuesday 22 July), covering preparedness, emergency response, evacuation, welfare, and recovery across multiple operational domains with a potential $50–100M investment case and 3–5 years of advisory follow-on. ADB's three core questions for the pitch are: (1) how the approach delivers all outputs in 12 weeks, (2) how BCG brings technical expertise across domains, and (3) what the advisory-only implementation support model looks like. Jamie owns point 4 (Philippines disaster ecosystem) on the 'seven things to get right' slide, the ecosystem approach snapshot, the implementation support model section, and is leading overall pitch material iteration with Luke. Key open questions include confirming the deadline driving the 12-week pace (to be asked at the pitch), finalising the Craig Fugate/Jakob Schrod expert addition via Elton, and ensuring Maxine is briefed on the approach slide before Tuesday. Elton flagged cost modeling as a rabbit hole to manage early by anchoring on a simple ambition-and-risk-appetite assumptions approach rather than benchmarking.

meetings.title: Djoey discussion
meetings.summary: Jamie decided to staff Djoey after a one-on-one conversation that revealed neutral framing of past experiences and genuine hunger — both positive signals. A precondition was set: an adult-to-adult conversation with Eric, which Djoey completed and even volunteered to help with Eric's all-hands. Key development targets are dramatically improving PSI, elevating critical thinking, and using KN rather than building credentials from scratch. The main risk flag is Djoey saying different things to different people, which Jamie plans to test directly this Friday. An interim CDC on Tuesday will discuss Djoey's staffing situation, and John confirmed he would not staff her again without conditions.

meetings.title: Discussion with CPFI
meetings.summary: Jamie joined a BCG pitch call with Chris and Cartini/Jules of CPFI, presenting BCG's approach to addressing the Philippine coconut industry's aging-tree and replanting crisis, drawing on analogous work in Brazil (orange/pasture) and India (Tamil Nadu coconut value chain). The key insight aligned on was that the demand side is relatively healthy and the Coco Levy Fund (~₱100B) is sufficient to fund replanting at scale — the core problem is unlocking and efficiently disbursing that capital through improved farmer registries, last-mile seedling distribution, and potential law amendments. BCG proposed a phased diagnostic starting with a province-level heat map, farmer productivity/economics analysis, and stakeholder coalition design (including PPP and private sector-led models like the Universal Leaf tobacco model). Next steps are for Cartini/Jules to review the deck and provide refinement feedback, for BCG to send commercials/investment figures, and to have an offline discussion on funding structure before reconvening in a few weeks.
